behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real vision-model moderation quality (explicit-content detection), which a mock classifier can trivially fake. The hold/not-publish branch should be asserted end-to-end after the real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit test images through the real moderation service using the production vision model endpoint (or staging equivalent) and assert each post ends in `held_for_review` and never reaches `published` state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is a robustness/quality claim about the text model’s real semantic handling of obfuscation, not just pipeline wiring. Mocking the classifier would likely hide true misses.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants against the real text moderation model via the service API, then assert each submission is flagged and held for human review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is orchestration/state transition across moderation modules and persistence/queueing, not model quality. Deterministic safe outputs from checkers are sufficient to expose publish-vs-hold/drop bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the moderation pipeline with deterministic ‘safe’ classifier adapters and a real local persistence/queue layer, submit posts, and assert final state is `published` with no missing records in hold/retry/dead-letter paths.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO over multiple agentic steps in sequence. Mocking model calls can hide real latency, contention, and sequencing overhead, so a full pipeline run with real model calls is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute timed end-to-end submissions containing both image and text through the real moderation pipeline (real model calls enabled) under representative peak load, and assert p95 completion from submit-to-final-decision is <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real failure semantics of an external dependency (timeouts/connection errors/5xx) and retry queue behavior. Pure mocks can miss transport-level and timeout-path bugs that cause accidental approval.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-endpoint unavailability (e.g., blackhole route or return persistent 503/timeouts) while submitting image posts; assert they transition to `retry_queued` and never to `approved/published` until a successful retry.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for borderline cases is driven by real model variability (and any stabilization logic). Mocked outputs would hide nondeterministic flips that this requirement is specifically about.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target time window using real vision-model inference, then assert decision consistency policy (e.g., identical held/publish outcome, or consistent cached decision) across attempts.
